_comment: Accepted or triaged Slither findings. Each entry must include a justification and review metadata. See docs/slither-static-analysis.md.

accepted.justification: Accepted false positive: this equality check is on deterministic contribution state and claimant identity, not a manipulable balance or timestamp value.
Accepted false positive: keccak256(bytes(a)) == keccak256(bytes(b)) is the standard Solidity pattern for string equality and is not a balance-comparison bug.
Accepted false positive: these strict equalities are bounded accounting and state invariants (dust == 0 fast path and claimed-vs-claimable counters), not exploitable token-balance comparisons.
Accepted false positive: the pre-storage external call is to the constructor-configured tokenDeploymentFactory, which is trusted deployment infrastructure rather than caller-controlled execution.
Pre-existing FundingVault dust-sweep equality pattern; ID changed after line shifts in HOK-1816 without changing the accepted risk.
Residual reentrancy-no-eth finding surfaced during HOK-1823 after adding nonReentrant guards to adjacent functions; no ETH is transferred in this code path and there is no fund safety risk.

Residual InfrastructureReserve batch payment finding after HOK-1829 role line shifts; accounting is updated before the ERC20 transfer and the function is protected by nonReentrant.
Residual DeltaVerifier registry-call finding after HOK-1829 role line shifts; contribution IDs are intentionally not consumed because the registry side effect is the canonical record.
Residual DeltaVerifier batch registry-call finding after HOK-1829 role line shifts; contribution IDs are intentionally not consumed because the registry side effect is the canonical record.
Residual InfrastructureReserve batch payment finding after HOK-1829 role line shifts; the function uses CEI accounting and nonReentrant before provider token transfers.
Residual InfrastructureReserve batch deposit finding after HOK-1829 role line shifts; the only post-transfer global accounting is cumulative statistics and the function is nonReentrant.
Residual InfrastructureReserve single deposit finding after HOK-1829 role line shifts; the function is nonReentrant and the flagged external call is the expected ERC20 transferFrom.
TokenDeploymentFactory emits after configuring freshly deployed HokusaiToken and HokusaiParams contracts; the only theoretical risk is event ordering and no mutable factory state is exposed.
InfrastructureReserve emergency withdrawal transfers to the configured treasury and then emits; the only theoretical risk is event ordering and no reserve accounting is updated after transfer.
HokusaiAMM buy is guarded by nonReentrant; the flagged external calls are ERC20 transfers and TokenManager minting with no ETH transfer and reserve accounting updated before mint emission.
HokusaiAMM fee deposit is guarded by nonReentrant; the external call is the expected ERC20 transferFrom and post-transfer state only records reserve accounting and graduation state.
HokusaiAMM treasury withdrawal transfers to the configured treasury and then emits; the only theoretical risk is event ordering and no reserve accounting is updated after transfer.
HokusaiAMMFactory createPoolWithParams records pool state before registering the pool and emitting; registry failure reverts the transaction and the only theoretical risk is event ordering.
HokusaiAMMFactory createPoolWithWhitelist emits after successful pool creation and whitelist attachment; the only theoretical risk is event ordering after trusted pool configuration.
HokusaiAMMFactory createPoolWithParamsAndWhitelist emits after successful pool creation and whitelist attachment; the only theoretical risk is event ordering after trusted pool configuration.
HokusaiAMMFactory pausePool emits only after the target pool pause succeeds; the external call is owner/pauser-gated and the only theoretical risk is event ordering.
HokusaiAMMFactory unpausePool emits only after the target pool unpause succeeds; the external call is owner-gated and the only theoretical risk is event ordering.
HokusaiAMMFactory pausePools emits after each successful bounded pool pause; calls are owner/pauser-gated and the only theoretical risk is event ordering.
HokusaiAMMFactory unpausePools emits after each successful bounded pool unpause; calls are owner-gated and the only theoretical risk is event ordering.
HokusaiAMMFactory pauseAllPools emits after each successful bounded pool pause; calls are owner/pauser-gated and the only theoretical risk is event ordering.
HokusaiAMM buy is nonReentrant and updates reserve accounting before minting; the PR only added a post-trade purchase event and does not introduce an unguarded value-transfer path.
HokusaiAMM depositFees is nonReentrant and unchanged by this PR; the new ID is baseline drift after nearby event declarations shifted Slither hashing.
HokusaiAMM withdrawTreasury is owner-gated and unchanged by this PR; the remaining theoretical risk is event ordering after a successful treasury transfer.

Factory pool creation now delegates deployment to the constructor-pinned HokusaiAMMPoolDeployer and then performs canonical ModelRegistry registration; both external hops are trusted infrastructure and no ETH is transferred in this path.
Factory pool creation performs trusted deployment and canonical registration before emitting events; the detector flags event ordering, but the owner-only path does not create a fund safety issue.
Factory pool creation emits after trusted deployer and registry calls; the flagged risk is limited to event ordering on an owner-only path and does not change canonical tracking or fund balances.
HokusaiAMMPoolDeployer deploys a new pool and immediately transfers ownership back to the factory before emitting; the detector only identifies possible event reordering, not a state safety issue.
Whitelist attachment runs only after factory ownership has been restored to the factory, and the detector is flagging owner-only post-call event ordering rather than an exploitable state transition.
Custom-params whitelist creation follows the same owner-only pattern as standard pool creation; the only flagged effect is event ordering after trusted internal infrastructure calls.
Single-pool pause is owner-or-pauser gated and forwards to a known pool before emitting; the detector reports theoretical event ordering only, with no unsafe post-call accounting.
Single-pool unpause is owner-only and only emits after forwarding to the known pool; this is an accepted event-ordering false positive, not a fund or authorization issue.
Batch pause loops over factory-tracked pools and emits after each pause; the detector flags event ordering on a governance-only path with no mutable accounting after the call.
Paginated pauseAllPools iterates over factory-tracked pools and emits after each pause; this is the same governance-only event-ordering pattern already accepted elsewhere in the repo.
Paginated unpauseAllPools is owner-only and touches only factory-tracked pools; the detector is identifying possible event ordering, not an exploitable reentrancy condition.
